steps.title: Trace Malthus to biology
steps.content: Darwin's notebook connects species' struggle for survival to Malthus, whose economic essay argued that population growth outpaces food production. A later 1838 notebook uses similar population-pressure language, suggesting Darwin had applied that economic idea to biology by then.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice A adds a date as late as 1858 without support. Choice B reverses the influence by making Malthus depend on animal behavior. Choice D says economists had already stated natural selection in the same terms, which the passage does not show. The remaining inference matches the Malthus connection. The answer is C.
